description_long: This object is a cylindrical cigarette with a white paper body and a conical tip that is darkened and ashy, indicating it has been partially smoked. The tip features a mix of black and orange-red colors, simulating burning embers and ash. The body is smooth and uniform, lacking any visible branding, text, or decorative elements. The cigarette does not have a visible filter, and the entire length appears to be a single, continuous tube. The surface of the paper is matte and slightly rough, typical of cigarette rolling paper. The object is small and lightweight, consistent with a standard hand-rolled or manufactured cigarette.
description: A white cylindrical cigarette with a dark, ashy, and glowing ember tip, lacking a filter or branding. The body is smooth and matte, with visible burn marks at the tip.
description_view_0: The cigarette is seen from the side, showing the white body and the ashy, glowing tip with orange and black coloration.
description_view_1: Similar to view 0, the ashy tip is visible, with the orange ember pattern slightly shifted, and the white body remains featureless.
description_view_2: The white cylindrical body is prominent, with the ashy, ember tip clearly visible at the top.
description_view_3: The ashy, glowing tip is most directly visible, with the orange and black pattern at its peak, and the white body below.
attribute_burn_mark: The tip of the cigarette is darkened with black and orange discoloration, indicating burn marks and charring.
